schema_source: ToolCatalog owns the live runtime tool surfaces. This JSON publishes those names plus the canonical MCP contract fingerprint for scripts, docs, and release artifacts.
compatibility_policy: Version 3 intentionally replaces the legacy verbose contract shape with discovery_policy, claim_policy, resources, and tool_schemas. Consumers should migrate by reading role tool names from discovery_policy and per-tool arguments from tool_schemas.
human_text_policy: Human-facing long text fields are Markdown unless a field is explicitly a compact label, status, identifier, branch, PR URL, or other machine-readable value. Dashboard renderers must not turn raw Markdown HTML into executable or trusted HTML.

discovery_policy.shared_tool_scope_policy: Shared tool names are intentionally exposed in both worker and architect sessions. The live handler applies role-specific authorization and target scope; shared tool_schemas rows use the strict cross-role shape while live tools/list remains authoritative for role-specific compact worker schemas.

shared_tool_scope.add_comment: Workers may omit target_kind and target_id to comment on their current WorkPackage. Architects must pass explicit target_kind and target_id because architect sessions can span multiple targets.
shared_tool_scope.list_comments: Workers may omit target_kind and target_id to list comments on their current WorkPackage. Architects must pass explicit target_kind and target_id because architect sessions can span multiple targets.
shared_tool_scope.resolve_blocker: Workers may resolve blockers in their current WorkPackage scope. Architects may resolve blockers for policy-scoped descendant WorkPackages visible from their claimed WorkRequest/phase scope.

discovery_policy.authorization_policy: tools/list is session-shape discovery, not authorization. Calls still require the matching local claim, role, capability, scope, lifecycle state, and handler-specific validation.

claim_policy.reclaim_policy: Replaying the same id heartbeats the current lease. Stale leases may be reclaimed with audit evidence; paused leases and another active owner are rejected. claim_local_architect_assignment may recover stale handoff scope only when the ledger proves exactly one matching WorkRequest, repo, base branch, anchor, phase, and architect grant; remaining phase_scope_not_available errors include missing_evidence plus an operator repair action, and archived or manually completed WorkRequests return work_request_terminal.
claim_policy.secret_policy: Agent-facing tools, prompts, launch payloads, and docs do not require raw grant secrets, private handoff metadata, secret stores, work keys, or helper scripts.

simplified_tools.name: attach_branch
simplified_tools.fallback: Pass branch explicitly when the branch pattern is templated or absent.

simplified_tools.name: current_work_request_lifecycle_tools
inferred: work_request_id from the claimed current architect WorkRequest
simplified_tools.fallback: Pass work_request_id explicitly to assert the same current WorkRequest; cross-scope targets are still denied.

retained_explicit_id_reasons: Delivery closeout, dispatch, guidance filters, runtime cleanup, and worker-key revocation may omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has one current WorkRequest; planned_slice_id remains explicit for product delivery truth, cleanup evidence, and linked runtime authority.
Runtime cleanup and planned-slice worker-key revocation fail closed when duplicate planned-slice links share the same WorkPackage runtime.
Superseded closeout keeps successor_planned_slice_id and optional successor_work_package_id because the caller is naming a cross-slice successor relation.
Worker-key revocation keeps grant_id because revocation is a concurrency-sensitive runtime operation on one live worker grant.
Architect reads that intentionally target sibling WorkRequests, status/question tools, durable decision tools, and package tools still require explicit target ids.

Architect package tools such as read_child_status, approve_scope_expansion, prepare_work_package_worktree, and cleanup_work_package_worktree still require WorkPackage ids because architect sessions may see more than one package.

sync_pr() is only a refresh path for the already attached PR when state changed after attachment or the attach call did not include current-state metadata required by policy; explicit PR identity or recovery payloads are repair fallbacks.

tool_schemas.name: set_status
tool_schemas.blocker_closeout_policy: When active blockers exist, provide blocker_closeout with decision=resolved plus a resolution, or decision=still_active to keep blockers active after the transition attempt.

tool_schemas.name: add_comment
tool_schemas.worker_compact_policy: Bound worker tools/list accepts body-only to comment on the current WorkPackage. Architects still pass target_kind and target_id.

tool_schemas.name: list_comments
tool_schemas.worker_compact_policy: Bound worker tools/list accepts an empty argument object to list comments on the current WorkPackage. Architects still pass target_kind and target_id.

tool_schemas.name: attach_branch
tool_schemas.worker_compact_policy: Bound worker tools/list requires head_sha only when the current WorkPackage has a literal branch_pattern; pass branch for templated or absent branch patterns.

tool_schemas.name: attach_pr
tool_schemas.current_state_policy: May include current check, review, or merge metadata. That attachment satisfies current PR state when the metadata proves the attached PR and current head.

tool_schemas.name: sync_pr
tool_schemas.attached_pr_policy: Refreshes only the already attached PR. Bound workers may call sync_pr() with no arguments; top-level current-state fields refine the refresh, and explicit PR identity or recovery payloads are repair fallbacks.

tool_schemas.name: attach_review_suite_result
tool_schemas.fallback_policy: If round_id resolves to passing local Review Suite state, suite/profile/lane/head/status/verdict/summary/anchor are inferred. Omit round_id when using verbose fallback fields; suite must identify Review Suite.

tool_schemas.name: mark_ready
tool_schemas.blocker_closeout_policy: When active blockers exist, provide blocker_closeout with decision=resolved plus a resolution, or decision=still_active to leave blockers active and keep readiness blocked.

tool_schemas.name: mint_child_worker_key
tool_schemas.template_policy: Optional template may set capabilities, expires_at, and claimed_by only.

tool_schemas.name: read_work_request_delivery_board
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked.

tool_schemas.name: reconcile_work_request
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked.

tool_schemas.name: cleanup_work_request_planned_slice_runtime
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked.
tool_schemas.cleanup_policy: Requires superseded or abandoned delivery evidence before cleanup. Revokes linked worker grants, releases non-paused current local claim leases, clears recoverable worker MCP session bindings for the linked WorkPackage, and records audit progress. Paused leases and fresh active AgentRun evidence fail closed. Duplicate planned-slice links to one WorkPackage fail closed before package-wide runtime mutation.

tool_schemas.name: record_planned_slice_delivery
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked.
tool_schemas.evidence_policy: pr_merged requires pr_url and pr_merged_at; linked packages also require merge_commit_sha. completed_no_pr requires no_pr_evidence. superseded requires successor_planned_slice_id and superseded_reason. abandoned requires abandoned_rationale.

tool_schemas.name: revoke_planned_slice_worker_key
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked. Duplicate planned-slice links to one WorkPackage fail closed before package-wide runtime mutation.

tool_schemas.name: list_guidance_requests
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: When work_package_id and work_request_id are omitted from a current WorkRequest claim, guidance is filtered to linked packages for the current WorkRequest. Explicit work_request_id filters require read:work_request.

tool_schemas.name: add_work_request_planned_slice
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked.

tool_schemas.name: upsert_work_request_product_plan_node_content
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked. New nodes require title.

tool_schemas.name: move_work_request_product_plan_node
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked. Pass parent_id null or empty to move the node to the WorkRequest root; omit parent_id to keep the current parent.

tool_schemas.name: set_work_request_product_plan_node_completion
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked. Terminal marks require blocker_closeout when descendant WorkPackages have active blockers.

tool_schemas.name: move_work_request_planned_slice_to_product_node
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked.

tool_schemas.name: approve_work_request_planned_slice
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked.

tool_schemas.name: skip_work_request_planned_slice
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked.

tool_schemas.name: mark_work_request_sliced
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked.

tool_schemas.name: dispatch_work_request_planned_slice
tool_schemas.current_work_request_policy: Omit work_request_id only when the claimed architect session has exactly one current WorkRequest. Explicit ids are still scope-checked.
tool_schemas.response_policy: Returns labeled worker_execution/product_coordinate ids plus worker_bootstrap for claim_local_assignment with work_package_id and optional claimed_by.
